admission_type: Free access (non-restricted) master's degree subject to verification of curricular requirements and a free assessment of the adequacy of personal preparation. There is no competitive entrance test. Non-EU candidates residing abroad must pass UniGeApply pre-evaluation.

academic_requirements.summary: Admission requires a recognised bachelor's (or higher) degree in a relevant engineering/computer-science field with adequate knowledge in core subject areas, followed by a free verification of personal preparation.
academic_requirements.prior_degree_or_diploma: A bachelor's degree of at least 180 CFU/ECTS, a Laurea, or an equivalent qualification (including a foreign degree recognised as suitable) in Information Engineering, Computer Science, or Industrial Engineering.
academic_requirements.subject_requirements: Adequate knowledge is required in: mathematical analysis, geometry, physics, mathematical physics, electronics, informatics, automatic/control systems, mechanics, telecommunications, and sensor and actuator technologies.
academic_requirements.minimum_grade_or_cgpa: [uncertain] On UniGeApply, Robotics Engineering lists a minimum CGPA of 70% of the maximum obtainable for the fall (non-EU/visa) session, and 60% for selected countries in the spring session.
academic_requirements.preparation_assessment: The adequacy of personal preparation is verified through a free-of-charge assessment of the curricular requirements; there is no competitive entrance test. Details are on the dedicated admission page.
academic_requirements.international_qualification: Foreign qualifications recognised as suitable are accepted; non-EU candidates residing abroad apply through UniGeApply for pre-evaluation before enrolment.

accepted_proofs: A Medium-of-Instruction (MOI) certificate / official document showing prior studies were taught in English
exemption_rules: An English certificate is not required if the language of instruction of the applicant's studies is English and this is certified by an official document or letter from the university.
language_requirements.assessment_or_upload_stage: English B2 proof is part of the curricular/personal-preparation verification; for non-EU applicants the certificate or MOI is uploaded during UniGeApply pre-evaluation. Certificates should generally be not older than 3 years.

entry_exam_or_test.test_name: Verification of adequacy of personal preparation (no competitive entrance test).
entry_exam_or_test.applies_to: All applicants holding the curricular requirements for the Robotics Engineering master's.
entry_exam_or_test.format: A free-of-charge documentary/qualitative assessment of curricular requirements and personal preparation (based on CV, transcripts, motivation letter, English certificate). No written or competitive entrance examination.
entry_exam_or_test.dates: [uncertain] No fixed institution-wide test date; the assessment is conducted within the admission/pre-evaluation procedure.
entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: No numerical pass score published; the assessment verifies adequacy of preparation rather than producing a competitive ranking.

rounds: Spring session for EU citizens and non-EU residents in Italy (UniGeApply): opens 9 April 2026; master's degrees close 28 August 2026.
The course deadlines page references a 9 January 2026 date for EU-based / Italy-based degree holders, which appears to belong to a prior/transitional cycle rather than the 2026/27 spring window.
application_deadline_eu.enrolment_deadline: First-year pre-registration/enrolment runs from 9 April to 30 November 2026 (until 10 March 2027 with late payment); confirmation of enrolment from 15 July 2026 to 30 November 2026 (until 10 March 2027 with late payment).
application_deadline_eu.notes: Free-access master's, so EU/resident applicants are not competitively ranked. Fee instalments for A.Y. 2026/2027: second by 10 December 2026, third by 23 June 2027.

rounds: Fall session for non-EU candidates requiring a visa (UniGeApply): opens 26 November 2025; institution-wide close 20 March 2026; pre-evaluation results by 22 May 2026.
The course deadlines page references a generic 'March' date for non-EU degree holders consistent with the fall close.
application_deadline_non_eu.visa_or_pre_enrolment_deadline: After UniGeApply eligibility, visa applicants complete the Universitaly pre-enrolment and then apply for the study visa, per the central UniGe international enrolment calendar.
application_deadline_non_eu.early_closure: None published for Robotics Engineering. The only fall-session early closures listed for A.Y. 2026/27 are Medical-Pharmaceutical Biotechnology (13 Feb 2026), Maritime Science and Technology bachelor (18 Feb 2026), and Computer Engineering bachelor (8 Mar 2026).
application_deadline_non_eu.notes: Non-EU visa applicants must use the fall session; the A.Y. 2026/27 fall window has closed (20 Mar 2026). UniGeApply allows 1 to 2 assessment requests per applicant.

required_documents.notes: Submitted for the qualitative assessment of personal preparation.

required_documents.document_name: Bachelor's degree certificate (final or provisional) and transcript of records
required_documents.notes: Bachelor of at least 180 CFU/ECTS in Information Engineering, Computer Science, or Industrial Engineering; transcript lists exams and credits.
Specifically required for Robotics Engineering; a template is provided on the programme's course page.

required_documents.document_name: English language certificate (B2) or Medium-of-Instruction (MOI) certificate
required_documents.notes: B2-level English proof (e.g. TOEFL/IELTS/Cambridge); MOI accepted when prior studies were taught in English.
Passport is the only accepted ID for non-EU applicants on UniGeApply (photo as JPG, supporting documents as PDF up to 2 MB).

required_documents.document_name: Legalization / CIMEA Statement of Verification and Declaration of Value or CIMEA Statement of Comparability
required_documents.notes: Required for non-EU visa applicants with a foreign qualification to complete enrolment.

source_quotes.quote: ROBOTICS ENGINEERING ... MASTER'S DEGREE (D.M. 1649/2023) ... Degree class LM-32 (Computer Systems Engineering) ... Genova ... completely taught in English ... 120 CFU, 2 years

a Bachelor degree (of at least 180 CFU/ECTS) ... in Information Engineering or Computer Science or Industrial Engineering ... adequate knowledge in mathematical analysis, geometry, physics, mathematical physics, electronics, Informatics, automatic, mechanics, telecommunications, sensor and actuator technologies

CEFR/QCER B2 level ... TOEFL (CBT 220, PBT 550, IBT 80), Cambridge B2 First Test (160), IELTS (6.5), TOEIC (800), Trinity (ISE II), IGCSE (C) ... This document is not required if the language of instruction of your studies is in English and this is certified by an official document or letter from the University
Robotics Engineering: minimum CGPA 70% (fall session) / 60% for select countries (spring session); Motivation letter (template provided)
Fall session (visa applicants): opens 26 November 2025, closes 20 March 2026, results by 22 May 2026. Spring session (residents): opens 9 April 2026, master's close 28 August 2026.

uncertainty_notes: Teaching language confirmed: 'completely taught in English' (LM-32, Genoa); course code 11963 confirmed on the official corsi.unige.it page.

The CGPA thresholds (70% fall / 60% spring for select countries) and the 'motivation letter (template provided)' requirement come from the UniGeApply non-EU master's pre-evaluation summary; confirm against the live listing.

The per-programme course deadlines page mixes year labels (a 'March 2025' non-EU date and a '9 January 2026' EU date) that appear to be from a prior/transitional cycle; the authoritative 2026/27 windows used here are the institution-wide UniGeApply fall (visa) and spring (resident) sessions.
No fall-session early closure is published for Robotics Engineering.
